riddle:
1. Seek the 2Rule Wikipedia page and read its opening: in what year was this naming convention introduced?

2. There exists an article about the numeric codes assigned to coordinate reference systems; on that page, how many constituent parts are listed as composing a full spatial reference specification (think ellipsoid, datum, projection, origin and unit)?

3. What prime number comes immediately after the count you just uncovered — before moving on, report the next prime after clue 2's value.

4. If you follow the overview of spatial reference frameworks, note how many language links are shown at the top of that article.

5. The article whose name embeds the year used for Great Britain’s classic national grid contains that year in its title; what year does it include?

6. A neighbourhood north of Chennai takes its name from a colour; find that locality’s page and record how many metres above sea level its ground sits.

7. The flagship high-priority Indian capital train has a page that records when its first services began; read that entry and note the date of its inaugural run.

8. (As a simple census question) How many people currently live in the central European country bounded by Slovakia and Austria — report its population figure.

9. Take the population number you just found and compute the sum of its distinct prime factors; supply that sum.

10. Search for the semi–high-speed Indian service often described as the fastest scheduled intercity train and read how frequently it runs; what service frequency is given?

11. Before leaving that page, count how many vowels appear in the train’s own name and give that count.

12. The main railway station serving Ajmer lists its layout details; in the section describing platforms, how many platforms are noted?

13. Look up the coastal capital of Peru’s principal plaza and, using local place searches, count how many parks are shown nearby.

14. The major rail hub in the capital of Jharkhand provides geographic coordinates in its infobox; what coordinates are listed there?

15. A British Army Air Corps airfield in Hampshire also gives coordinates in its infobox; record those coordinates next.

16. Convert an 8 °C temperature to its Fahrenheit-scale offset by multiplying by 1.8 and taking the integer part; what integer result do you obtain?

17. Now take the integer you just produced and determine how many positive divisors it has in total; report that divisor count.

18. The model 412 utility helicopter’s page lists the month and year of its first flight; what month and year are given?

19. Read the entry for the Twin Huey family of helicopters and name the armed conflict during which this variant first entered service.

20. Again, consult a current country population source: what population is listed for the country bordering Slovakia and Austria? (this repeats clue 8’s query for the same nation)

21. Reduce the population figure you just obtained to its digital root by iteratively summing digits until a single digit remains; report that final digit.

22. Finally, compute (clue6 + clue17 + clue21) % 10 and give the single-digit result (0–9).

metadata.theme: Mapping movement — a short trail from coordinate systems and spatial identifiers to real-world routing, elevation and weather for rail and air transport
